incident_2026_07_21.summary: Perceived total data loss was actually a 21:28 host reboot caused by cascading Ubuntu upgrades applied while a GNOME session and live agent sessions were running. The filesystem was 100% intact; only live processes and tmpfs state were lost.
timeline: 06:31 unattended-upgrade installed linux-image-7.0.0-28 and REMOVED the running 7.0.0-22 kernel (kernel swap under a live session)
19:44 aptdaemon upgraded snapd 2.75->2.76, accountsservice, google-chrome, and vscode, destabilizing the live desktop
21:28 reboot into 7.0.0-28; all live processes ended
facts: Filesystem 100% intact (/dev/nvme1n1p2, 3.6T, 26% used); 498G RAM with zero OOM; no NVIDIA Xid — not a hardware crash
journald is volatile (no /var/log/journal), so prior-boot logs were unrecoverable
/run/user/1001/yazelix/profile-runtime (CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR, codex) is tmpfs and was wiped
Durable transcripts at ~/.claude/projects (409 jsonl) survived with pre-reboot mtimes
The host itself was healthy: drdave updated and rebooted with no issues afterward — the defect was LifeOS being coupled to the host session lifecycle

failure_modes.summary: unattended-upgrades kernel swap under a live session (installed 7.0.0-28, removed the running 7.0.0-22)
failure_modes.root_cause: Host update policy is ungoverned: kernel replacement proceeds with no awareness of active LifeOS work, making an eventual reboot mandatory mid-session.

failure_modes.summary: In-session snapd/accountsservice/desktop-package upgrades destabilize the live desktop
failure_modes.root_cause: aptdaemon and snap refresh apply desktop-breaking packages during active work; there is no hold window tied to session liveness.

failure_modes.summary: Durable agent runtime parasitized on tmpfs profile-runtime (/run/user/1001/yazelix/profile-runtime)
failure_modes.root_cause: The generated profile runtime tree (CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR, CODEX_HOME, yazelix state) lives on the host systemd /run tmpfs, so every reboot wipes durable-class agent state — the direct cause of the 2026-07-21 session loss.

failure_modes.summary: Home residuals as active owners (~/.claude transcripts, ~/.local/share tools, ~/FlexNetOS runner _work)
failure_modes.root_cause: XDG defaults and the runner service point durable tool state at home-owned paths outside the single Nix-profile frontdoor, violating path-law and scattering state across owners.

failure_modes.summary: Host docker/kvm stacks outside Nix (Omada moby container 98ade093; libvirtd/qemu-kvm boot units enabled)
failure_modes.root_cause: The Omada network controller runs as an APT-installed dockerd container with an overlay-backed Mongo datadir, and unused libvirt/qemu units start at boot — services and state outside declared Nix supervision.

failure_modes.summary: Volatile journald leaves no prior-boot forensics
failure_modes.root_cause: No persistent /var/log/journal exists, so update-event and crash forensics vanish at reboot, blinding incident analysis and update governance.

failure_modes.summary: Interactive agent shell runs unsandboxed directly on the host
failure_modes.root_cause: The session shares the host mount/pid namespaces (no envelope), so Nix provides hermetic binaries but no runtime isolation; commands mutate the host directly.

failure_modes.summary: Reboot ends live processes with no automatic re-attach
failure_modes.root_cause: The shared kernel honestly ends processes at reboot, and no boot-triggered unit re-materializes the envelope and re-attaches durable state, so recovery is manual.
